title: Ce ne așteaptă în 2026
summary: The article analyzes Romania's political landscape heading into 2026, focusing on the Constitutional Court crisis exemplified by judge Giani Stan still being a doctoral student while serving on the court. It argues that the resolution of the judiciary crisis will shape the broader political balance between PSD, Premier Bolojan's camp, and President Nicușor Dan's circle, and that competence and honesty will be decisive factors in 2026.
talking_points: Constitutional Court judge Giani Stan is still a doctoral student, symbolizing competence problems in Romania's judiciary
The judiciary crisis resolution will determine the political power balance, with PSD's bureaucratic allies in the magistracy potentially enabling Grindeanu's rise to premiership
The 2025 transition from Ciucă-Ciolacu to Bolojan and from Iohannis to Nicușor Dan represented progress toward honesty and pro-Western orientation
Without competent public policy decisions following the emergency measures adopted since May 2025, the modest political progress will not hold
AUR and SOS Romania operate on the fringe, making delusional accusations about USR controlling Recorder and the judiciary
author: Cristian Preda
